findings: The watchdog loop is not approvable as a robust working cron/watchdog loop yet.
A direct forced run of docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py did start and produced updated status artifacts, proving the entrypoint is executable.

That forced run did not complete within roughly 2 minutes of observation and remained hung in a child ralph_docs_agentic_review.py process when manually terminated, so actual bounded completion of the whole watchdog loop was not demonstrated.
The updated status artifacts show concise reporting and coherent machine-readable/markdown output for a failure state.
Lock behavior is partly acceptable: nested runner/verifier calls fast-skipped with exit 75 and explicit global-lock messages while the top-level watchdog held the shared lock.

Runtime health is distinct from docs-content health here: docs content still reports BROKEN/fail, which is allowed for this verification task, but the runtime loop itself must still finish and report. That finish criterion was not proven.

Current runtime state shows consecutiveFailures incrementing and docs_stack_temp_watchdog_status.{json,md} updating to fingerprint 948ff14f95e75559, so artifact freshness/reporting exists even though end-to-end completion remains unproven.
proof: crontab currently contains only comment markers for the Ralph docs watchdog/verifier and no active crontab command lines for job id c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80, so scheduled execution was not evidenced from crontab itself.
Observed process after start: parent pid 1255221 still running with child 'python3 .../ralph_docs_agentic_review.py'; lock file present at agents/docs_quality/.docs_loop.lock.
Observed updated status file timestamp: docs_stack_temp_watchdog_status.md/json checkedAtUtc 2026-05-20 21:16:00 UTC.
Observed failure artifact content: runner/verifier nested invocations exited 75 with 'SKIP: another Ralph docs loop process already holds the global lock'.
Observed concise status reasons: ralph_docs_check.py failed; agentic status is not pass; agentic loopHealthy is not true; agentic still says the user should need to repeat this.
Manual termination was required for the forced run session after prolonged no-output wait, so bounded completion is not yet verified.
blockers: Need proof of actual scheduled execution for cron task c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80; current crontab output shows comments only, not active watchdog lines.
Need at least one observed end-to-end forced or scheduled run that exits on its own within an acceptable bound and leaves final status artifacts without manual kill.
Agentic review appears able to stall long enough to prevent demonstrating robust bounded runtime behavior; timeout/termination handling may need hardening or separate instrumentation.
